steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the main purpose of the passage. Notice the time markers: 'Every few days' and 'In the afternoons, she would' — both signal habitual, repeated action, not a one-time event. The passage centers on Abuela Evila's ritual of washing Élida's hair with lemon water as something that happened over and over during Grande's childhood.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘To give an example of a typical interaction between Grande's siblings’: Mago and Carlos appear only as observers hiding behind a bush; the passage is about Abuela Evila and Élida, not sibling interactions. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘To describe a regular occurrence from Grande's childhood’: 'Every few days' and the repeated-action phrasing make this a recurring scene, exactly what 'regular occurrence' captures. ✓
  • Choice C — ‘To illustrate how Grande's relationship with Élida grew over time’: the passage shows a static, frozen-in-memory scene; no development of any relationship is described. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘To explain how Grande felt about a location where she spent time as a child’: the passage describes the ritual, not Grande's feelings about a place. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
